Whatever \(P\) as above, we will note \(a_p\) and \(a'_p\) the images in \(P(\mathscr{F})\) from \(a\) and \(a'\). Lemma 9.1 follows from the following lemma, in which we take for \(P\) the projective system indexed by \(\{0\}\) and reduced to the final object of \(\mathcal{S}\).

blocks.title: Lemma
blocks.html:
Lemma 9.2

Whatever \(P\) is as highest satisfying \(a_p \neq a'_p\), there exists \(Q\) refining \(P\), verifying \(a_Q \neq a'_Q\), and such that, whatever the overlap \(f_i : V_i \to V\) and \(f \in Q(V)\), \(f\) or in the image of one of \(Q(V_i)\).
We will first prove:

blocks.title: Lemma
blocks.html:
Lemma 9.3

Let \(P = (U_i)_{i \in I}\) verify the hypotheses of Lemma 9.2, \(f_i : V_i \to V\) a finite overlap in \(\mathcal{S}\), and \(f \in P(V)\). There is \(Q\) refining \(P\), checking \(a_Q \neq a'_Q\) and such that the image of \(f\) in \(Q(V)\) is in the image of one of \((V_i)\).

There are \(i_0 \in I\) and \(f' \in \mathrm{Hom}(U_{i_0},V)\) which define \(f\). For \(i \geq i_0\), let us put \(U_{i,k} = U_i \times_V V_k\), this fiber product being defined by \(f'\). We know that \((V_k)\) is a cover of \(V\), so \(U_{i,k}\) is a cover of \(U_i\); the arrow following will be injective (for \(i > i_0\)): \[ \mathscr{F}(U_i) \to \prod_k \mathscr{F}(U_{i,k}) \] Passing to the limit, given that the finished products commute to the limits filtering inductive, we see that \[ P(\mathscr{F}) \to \prod_k \varinjlim \mathscr{F}(U_{i,k}) \] is injective, so that there exists \(k\) such that \(a_p\) and \(a'_p\) have distinct images in \(\varinjlim_{i>i_0} \mathscr{F}(U_{i,k})\). Let then be \(I_0 = \{i \mid i \geq i_0 \text{ et } i \in I\}\), and \(J = I \amalg I_0\). We orders \(J\) saying that \(j' \leq j''\) if the images of \(j'\) and \(j''\) in \(I\) satisfy to \(j' \leq j''\) and if we do not have \(j' \in I_0\), \(j'' \in I\). The \(U_i\) and \(U_{i,k}\) are indexed by \(J\), and form a refinement \(Q\) of \(P\), such that \(a_Q \neq a'_Q\), and the image of \(f\) in \(Q(V)\) or in the image of \(Q(V_k)\).

blocks.title: Lemma
blocks.html:
Lemma 9.4

Let \(P = (U_i)_{i \in I}\) verify the hypotheses of Lemma 9.2. There is \(Q\) refining \(P\), such that \(a_Q \neq a'_Q\), and such that for any finite covering \((V_k)\) of a open \(V\) of \(S\), and all \(f \in P(V)\), the image of \(f\) in \(Q(V)\) is in the image of one of \(Q(V_k)\).

Let \(E\) be the set of triples formed from an open \(V\) of \(S\), from an overlap finished \((V_k)\) of \(V\) and \(f \in P(V)\). Let \(\leq\) be a good order on \(E\) and \(\overline{E}\) the set deduced from \(E\) by adding a larger element, denoted \(\infty\). We will define by transfinite induction on \(e \in \overline{E}\) of the refinements \(Q_e\) of \(P\), verifying \(a_{Q_e} \neq a'_{Q_e}\) and such as
(i) if \(e' \leq e\), then \(Q_e\) refines \(Q_{e'}\).
(ii) if \(e = (V,(V_k),f)\), then the image of \(f\) without \(Q_e(V)\) is in the image of one of \(Q_e(V_k)\).

Assume the \(Q_e\) already defined for \(e' < e\). If \(e\) is the first element from \(E\), let's set \(Q'_e = P\). If \(e\) has a predecessor \(e-1\), let's set \(Q'_e = Q_{e-1}\). Otherwise, let \(Q'_e\) be the projective index set system \(\bigcup_{e'<e} I_{e'}\), which refines the \(Q_{e'}\), for \(e' < e\). In this case, we have \[ Q'_e(\mathscr{F}) = \varinjlim_{e'<e} Q_{e'}(\mathscr{F}) \] so that, in all cases, \(Q'_e\) refines the \(Q_{e'}\), for \(e' < e\) and checks \(a_{Q'_e} \neq a'_{Q'_e}\).
We obtain the required projective system \(Q_e\) by applying Lemma 9.3 to \(Q'_e\), and \(e = (V,(V_k),f)\) (resp. taking \(Q_e = Q'_e\) if \(e = \infty\)).
The projective system \(Q_\infty\) satisfies Lemma 9.4.
Lemma 9.4 allows us to define, by induction on \(n\), a sequence \(Q_n = (U_i)_{i \in I_n}\) of projective systems in \(\mathcal{S}\) such that
(ii)   \(Q_{n+1}\) refines \(Q_n\)
(iii)   \(a_{Q_n} \neq a'_{Q_n}\)
(iv)   Regardless of the overlap \(f_k : V_k \to V\) and \(f \in Q_n(V)\), the image of \(f\) in \(Q_{n+1}(V)\) is in the image of one of \(Q_{n+1}(V_k)\).
The projective system \(Q\), of set of indices the meeting of \(I_n\), which extends the different \(Q_n\), then verifies Lemma 9.2. The demonstration shows more than:

blocks.title: Corollary
blocks.html:
Corollary 9.5

Let \(\mathcal{S}\) be a site, in which the fiber products are representable and such that any overlap in \(\mathcal{S}\) admits a finite undercover. Either \(c\) Cardinal \(\sup(\aleph_0, \operatorname{card}(\mathrm{Fl}\,\mathcal{S}))\). So there exists a very dense set of points of \(\mathcal{S}\), such that

  1. (i)

    \(\operatorname{card}(X) \leq 2^c\)

  2. (ii)

    if \(x \in X\) and \(U \in \mathrm{Ob}\, \mathcal{S}\), then \(\operatorname{card}(U_x) \leq c\).
